---
title: "States Newsroom defense spending project"
format: html
  theme: journal
editor: visual
author: Sam Gauntt
---

```{r}
#loading packages and initial, messy data
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor) 


earmarks_24 <- read_csv("~/Documents/GitHub/SNDC-SamGauntt/defense/data/tabula-fy24 ndaa - cpf requests submitted for public posting 4.27.23.csv") |> clean_names() 

earmarks_25 <- read_csv("~/Documents/GitHub/SNDC-SamGauntt/defense/data/tabula-fy25 ndaa - cpf requests submitted for public posting 5.20.24.csv") |> clean_names() 

earmarks_26 <- read_csv("~/Documents/GitHub/SNDC-SamGauntt/defense/data/tabula-fy26_ndaa_-_cpf_requests_submitted_for_public_posting_7.7.25.csv") |> clean_names() 

earmarks_27 <- read_csv("~/Documents/GitHub/SNDC-SamGauntt/defense/data/tabula-fy27_ndaa_-_cpf_requests_submitted_for_public_posting_5.26.26.csv") |> clean_names() 


glimpse(earmarks_24)
glimpse(earmarks_25)
glimpse(earmarks_26)
glimpse(earmarks_27)



#lets add a column for fiscal year, and then bind into one dataframe for easier analysis. 

earmarks_24 <- earmarks_24 |> mutate(fiscal_year = "2024") 

earmarks_25 <- earmarks_25 |> mutate(fiscal_year = "2025")

earmarks_26 <- earmarks_26 |> mutate(fiscal_year = "2026")

earmarks_27 <- earmarks_27 |> mutate(fiscal_year = "2027") |> mutate(
    amount_requested_in_thousands_of_dollars = str_replace_all(amount_requested_in_thousands_of_dollars, ",", ""), 
    amount_requested_in_thousands_of_dollars = as.numeric(amount_requested_in_thousands_of_dollars)
  ) 

earmarks <- bind_rows(earmarks_24, earmarks_25, earmarks_26) 

write_csv(earmarks, "earmarks.csv") 


```

```{r}
#earmark totals

earmark_totals <- earmarks |> group_by(state) |>
  summarize(
    total_spending = sum(amount_requested_in_thousands_of_dollars)
  ) 

write_csv(earmark_totals, "earmark_states.csv") 

```

<iframe title="Florida received $2.4 billion in defense spending through earmarks from FY2024-26" aria-label="Choropleth map" id="datawrapper-chart-s2cTF" src="https://datawrapper.dwcdn.net/s2cTF/1/" scrolling="no" frameborder="0" style="width: 0; min-width: 100% !important; border: none;" height="527" data-external="1">

</iframe>

```{=html}
<script type="text/javascript">(function(){function e(){window.addEventListener(`message`,function(e){if(e.data[`datawrapper-height`]!==void 0){var t=document.querySelectorAll(`iframe`);for(var n in e.data[`datawrapper-height`])for(var r=0,i;i=t[r];r++)if(i.contentWindow===e.source){var a=e.data[`datawrapper-height`][n]+`px`;i.style.height=a}}})}e()})();</script>
```

```{r}
#defense contractor analysis 





```

```{r include=FALSE}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(gt)
```

```{r}
library(httr2)
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

# 1. Target a highly stable summary endpoint
url <- "https://api.usaspending.gov/api/v2/references/toptier_agencies/"

# 2. Execute a simple GET request (no complex JSON body required)
response <- request(url) %>%
  req_retry(max_tries = 3) %>%
  req_perform()

# 3. Parse the data
raw_data <- response %>% resp_body_json()

# 4. Filter for Department of Defense and extract details safely
agencies_df <- tibble(results = raw_data$results) %>%
  unnest_wider(results)

cat_url <- "https://api.usaspending.gov/api/v2/search/spending_by_category/recipient_duns/"

cat_payload <- list(
  filters = list(
    agencies = list(
      list(
        type = "awarding",
        tier = "toptier",
        name = "Department of Defense"
      )
    ),
    time_period = list(
      list(
        start_date = "2019-10-01",
        end_date = as.character(Sys.Date())
      )
    )
  ),
  limit = 50,
  page = 1
) 

cat_response <- request(cat_url) %>%
  req_body_json(cat_payload) %>%
  req_perform() 

cat_data <- cat_response %>% resp_body_json()

# Extract into Top 10 Data Table
top_10_defense <- tibble(results = cat_data$results) %>%
  unnest_wider(results) %>%
  select(
    recipient_name = name, 
    total_spending_received = amount
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    formatted_total = dollar(total_spending_received, scale = 1e-9, suffix = " B")
  ) 

print(top_10_defense)







```

```{r}
# 1. Define endpoint and years to evaluate
url <- "https://api.usaspending.gov/api/v2/search/spending_by_category/recipient_duns/"
fiscal_years <- c(2023, 2024, 2025)

# Helper function to query a specific fiscal year
get_defense_spending_by_year <- function(fy) {
  # Define dates based on federal fiscal year bounds (Oct 1 - Sept 30)
  start_date <- paste0(fy - 1, "-10-01")
  end_date <- paste0(fy, "-09-30")
  
  payload <- list(
    filters = list(
      agencies = list(
        list(type = "awarding", tier = "toptier", name = "Department of Defense")
      ),
      time_period = list(
        list(start_date = start_date, end_date = end_date)
      )
    ),
    limit = 100, # Top 100 companies per year to catch tech firms & traditional primes
    page = 1
  )
  
  response <- request(url) %>%
    req_body_json(payload) %>%
    req_retry(max_tries = 3) %>%
    req_perform()
  
  data <- response %>% resp_body_json()
  
  # Parse safely into a table
  tibble(results = data$results) %>%
    unnest_wider(results) %>%
    select(recipient_name = name, amount) %>%
    mutate(fiscal_year = fy)
}

# 2. Fetch data across all specified years and stack them
all_years_data <- map_dfr(fiscal_years, get_defense_spending_by_year)

# 3. Clean names and group similar entries (e.g., handling variations in casing)
cleaned_spending <- all_years_data %>%
  mutate(
    recipient_clean = str_to_upper(recipient_name),
    # Simplify Palantir variations if they appear with different sub-entities
    recipient_clean = if_else(str_detect(recipient_clean, "PALANTIR"), "PALANTIR TECHNOLOGIES", recipient_clean)
  ) %>%
  group_by(recipient_clean, fiscal_year) %>%
  summarise(total_amount = sum(amount, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# 4. Pivot wider to compute Year-over-Year Percentage Change
spending_trends <- cleaned_spending %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = fiscal_year, values_from = total_amount, names_prefix = "fy_") %>%
  # Fill missing fields with 0 if a company didn't make the top 100 in a certain year
  mutate(across(starts_with("fy_"), ~replace_na(., 0))) %>%
  # Calculate Percentage Changes
  mutate(
    pct_change_23_24 = ((fy_2024 - fy_2023) / fy_2023) * 100,
    pct_change_24_25 = ((fy_2025 - fy_2024) / fy_2024) * 100
  )

# 5. Filter to compare Palantir directly against traditional legacy primes
comparison_table <- spending_trends %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(recipient_clean, "PALANTIR|LOCKHEED|RAYTHEON|GENERAL DYNAMICS|BOEING|NORTHROP")
  ) %>%
  select(recipient_clean, fy_2023, fy_2024, fy_2025, pct_change_23_24, pct_change_24_25)

print(comparison_table)




```
